title: The Fabian Strategy

jokes.text: Who's Jackie?
jokes.setup: Jack introduces himself as 'Jack' to Liz
jokes.punchline: Liz mishears and asks who Jackie is
jokes.explanation: Classic Liz obliviousness, perfectly economical two-word setup and punchline

jokes.text: Sandwiches?
jokes.setup: Jack lists the 'three S's' of vacation: surf, sun, and...
jokes.punchline: Liz guesses 'sandwiches' instead of the obvious 'sex'
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Liz's food obsession and social awkwardness in one word

jokes.text: It was so warm you could pick fully cooked lobsters out of the water
jokes.setup: Jack describing how warm the vacation was
jokes.punchline: The absurd image of lobsters cooking themselves in the ocean
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist escalation that's both visual and absurd

jokes.text: Like a young Bo Derek stuffed with a Barry Goldwater
jokes.setup: Jack describing Avery as the most perfect woman
jokes.punchline: The bizarre combination of sex symbol and conservative politician
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Jack's character - refined and absurd political references in one line

jokes.text: You sound weird. Do you have a beard?
jokes.setup: Jack's voice sounds different over the phone
jokes.punchline: Liz's random guess that a beard would affect his voice
jokes.explanation: Classic Liz non-sequitur logic

jokes.text: No more making love on the beach surrounded by a privacy circle of English-trained butlers
jokes.setup: Jack lamenting the end of vacation
jokes.punchline: The absurdly specific and wealthy detail of butler privacy circles
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack wealth humor with specific absurd detail

jokes.text: Get this, my gynecologist committed suicide
jokes.punchline: Liz's matter-of-fact delivery of this dark news
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy delivered with perfect Liz casualness

jokes.text: And I'm back!
jokes.setup: Liz just revealed her gynecologist's suicide
jokes.punchline: Her cheerful declaration that she's back to normal after this trauma
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing - dark revelation followed by upbeat recovery

jokes.text: No, wait, actually that's a half-burned-down McDonald's
jokes.setup: Carol says he can see the St. Louis arch
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection but fairly standard mistake comedy

jokes.text: Who flies Newark to Atlantic City? Black bachelorette parties
jokes.setup: Liz questions the weird flight route
jokes.explanation: Oddly specific airline observation humor

jokes.text: Oh, I misdialed. I thought I was calling my nutritionist
jokes.setup: Tracy gives an inspiring speech to Liz about giving 110%
jokes.punchline: He meant to call someone else entirely
jokes.explanation: Classic Tracy randomness with good misdirection

jokes.text: We're already printing all of our internal memos on the back of my kids' art
jokes.setup: Discussion about cutting office supply costs
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific desperation humor

jokes.text: The Harry Potter theme park is a huge hit with both anglophiles and pedophiles
jokes.explanation: Dark corporate speak with perfect word pairing

jokes.text: The movie division has a James Cameron movie the whole world will see, whether they like it or not
jokes.explanation: Good media satire about blockbuster inevitability

jokes.text: Only NBC continues to be the engorged whitehead on the otherwise flawless face of Universal Media
jokes.punchline: The disgusting metaphor for NBC's status
jokes.explanation: Brilliant self-deprecating network humor with vivid imagery

jokes.text: Tracy's head size keeps changing
jokes.punchline: The impossible explanation for the costs
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy absurdity delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: Everyone must make eye contact with Miss Maroney at all times
jokes.explanation: Perfect diva demand that sets up visual gag

jokes.text: Like executive producer Ashton Kutcher, or secretary of state Hillary Clinton
jokes.punchline: Comparing Hillary Clinton to Ashton Kutcher
jokes.explanation: Brilliant political satire with perfect comparison

jokes.text: Eye contact
jokes.setup: Jenna's contract demand has been established
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate callback with visual comedy

jokes.text: I need you to go to the dry cleaners for me and find out how martinizing works. I've always been curious
jokes.punchline: The bizarrely specific curiosity about dry cleaning
jokes.explanation: Classic Tracy absurdity with perfect specificity

jokes.text: Then I need you to be back by noon to make the bathroom smell like sandalwood before I wreck it
jokes.punchline: The gross honesty about bathroom destruction
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor with Tracy's characteristic oversharing

jokes.text: I'm not Kenneth, sir. My name's Brian. Kenneth got fired at the end of last season
jokes.setup: Tracy calling the new page Kenneth
jokes.explanation: Functional exposition joke setting up Kenneth's absence

jokes.text: Of course. I knew that
jokes.setup: Tracy has been calling him Kenneth
jokes.explanation: Simple Tracy denial humor

jokes.text: Did you know that if you're a pilot, that Chili's will seat you right away, even if the pilot's dinner companion has just been yelling at the hostess?
jokes.setup: Liz talking about dating a pilot
jokes.punchline: The implication she was yelling at the hostess
jokes.explanation: Good Liz embarrassment humor with self-awareness

jokes.text: You are the Jackie O of our time
jokes.punchline: Jack's sarcastic comparison to Jackie Kennedy
jokes.explanation: Perfect sarcastic comparison highlighting Liz's gracelessness

jokes.text: Why would he? Those Starwinds are nice! I mean, the bathtubs are so much cleaner than at home
jokes.setup: Jack asking why Carol doesn't stay with her
jokes.punchline: Liz accidentally revealing her apartment's grossness
jokes.explanation: Classic Liz oversharing about her living conditions

jokes.text: It's almost as bad as 'climax'
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to another awkward word
jokes.explanation: Setup for callback, Jack's verbal squeamishness

jokes.text: Then one of you says, 'we should redecorate.' And the other one says, 'please, Avery, I'm using the commode right now.'
jokes.explanation: Good relationship humor with Jack's formal language

jokes.text: a reddish-brown shade called 'elk tongue'
jokes.setup: Jack describing his current wall color
jokes.explanation: Great absurd detail that becomes episode's MacGuffin

jokes.text: This is how I know you've never had an adult relationship
jokes.setup: Liz suggesting Jack just say no
jokes.explanation: Classic Jack superiority complex

jokes.text: Before you know it, she'll have me wearing jeans and reading fiction!
jokes.setup: Jack explaining the slippery slope of compromise
jokes.punchline: These mild activities as his worst nightmare
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack character moment - his horror at normalcy

jokes.text: If an apple and a feather fall at the same time...
jokes.explanation: Good Liz confusion moment mixing up concepts

jokes.text: He ran away, Lemon. Rather than engage in battle, he would retreat and retreat until the enemy grew fatigued and eventually made a mistake
jokes.explanation: Sets up the episode's central joke concept

jokes.text: Although I abhor it as a military strategy, it is the basis for all of my, uh, personal relationships
jokes.setup: Jack just explained a cowardly military strategy
jokes.punchline: He admits using it in relationships
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack character insight - formal cowardice

jokes.text: So Avery is your enemy. That sounds healthy
jokes.setup: Jack describing his relationship strategy as military
jokes.punchline: Liz's dry observation about the implications
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan response to Jack's absurdity

jokes.text: Meeting someone in a hotel room twice a month is not a relationship... just ask any hooker
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation with slightly crude comparison

jokes.text: I was going to say 'climax.' Aah!
jokes.punchline: Jack's physical reaction to his own word aversion
jokes.explanation: Good callback to his word aversion with physical comedy

jokes.text: It takes people and turns them into amounts of money
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna narcissism - loving the wrong thing

jokes.text: Except for Tracy, I'm the most person on the show!
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jenna line - her ego breaking language itself

jokes.text: You should fire Grace from wardrobe. She doesn't do anything
jokes.punchline: Jenna immediately throwing someone under the bus
jokes.explanation: Sets up the firing sequence

jokes.text: Come on, you'd have to be a heartless monster... I'll do it
jokes.setup: Liz describing how awful it would be to fire Grace
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection showing Liz's ruthless side

jokes.text: But I've been here since the Jack Paar show
jokes.explanation: Solid reference humor about NBC history

jokes.text: Your health insurance will remain in effect until the end of this sentence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant corporate cruelty joke with perfect timing

jokes.text: What a shank job
jokes.explanation: Good character detail with period-appropriate slang

jokes.text: Kenneth, I knew you'd come back! Let me smell your head
jokes.explanation: Classic Tracy inappropriate behavior

jokes.text: This is a woman's blazer from a very expensive blazer shop called Rico's
jokes.setup: Brian correcting Tracy about his identity
jokes.punchline: The oddly specific blazer shop detail
jokes.explanation: Weird specific detail humor

jokes.text: Just like my kidneys did to my lungs that time
jokes.setup: Tracy talking about his heart playing tricks on his eyes
jokes.explanation: Classic Tracy medical nonsense

jokes.text: Should I get my rainbow wig out of storage?
jokes.setup: Tracy asking if he's going crazy again
jokes.punchline: The specific prop for his insanity
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy backstory detail

jokes.text: Like the World Cup
jokes.setup: Liz telling him to say 'this is not real, I am in control'
jokes.explanation: Classic Tracy non-sequitur

jokes.text: Would you rather have to start every sentence you say for the rest of your life with Urkel's catchphrase, 'Did I do that?' Or be Siamese twins with Sharon Stone for a year?
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist game setup

jokes.text: Did I do that? Carol!
jokes.setup: The Urkel choice was just presented
jokes.explanation: Immediate callback with perfect timing

jokes.text: Try five miles high. And no, I have not
jokes.explanation: Good pilot-specific humor

jokes.text: No, but once, when I was in the Air Force, I saw Mr. T in a Pizza Hut
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection from UFOs to celebrity

jokes.text: Yeah, but with a lot more volleyball
jokes.setup: Asked if Air Force was like Top Gun
jokes.punchline: The emphasis on volleyball over flying
jokes.explanation: Good reference humor about Top Gun

jokes.text: Sucked a mechanic into my engine once
jokes.setup: Asked if he ever killed anyone
jokes.punchline: The casual description of accidental death
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered with perfect casualness

jokes.text: I'll just have to run home first and take some plates out of the bathroom
jokes.setup: Carol asking to stay at her place
jokes.punchline: Revealing she has plates in the bathroom
jokes.explanation: Classic Liz gross living situation reveal

jokes.text: The lady said they're all booked up for something called Jackfest
jokes.setup: All the Starwind hotels are booked
jokes.explanation: Reveals Jack's manipulation

jokes.text: A middle-aged woman saying 'dude stuff'... is that on my sadness scavenger hunt?
jokes.punchline: Jack's concept of a sadness scavenger hunt
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jack cruelty with perfect concept

jokes.text: I'm like that woman on the Food Network whose husband only comes home on the weekends, and she spends the rest of her time eating and drinking with her gay friends
jokes.explanation: Sets up the Barefoot Contessa running gag

jokes.text: Her name is the Barefoot Contessa, Lemon, and you will never be like her, starting with the barefoot part
jokes.setup: Liz comparing herself to Ina Garten
jokes.explanation: Good Jack put-down with specific detail

jokes.text: I'll have you know that I wore sandals this summer, over socks... in a dream
jokes.setup: Jack saying she'll never be barefoot
jokes.punchline: The pathetic escalation to dream footwear
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz pathetic escalation with great structure

jokes.text: No, you do not exist! I'm in control of this!
jokes.punchline: His attempt to use Liz's advice
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier advice

jokes.text: This is one of the three things in the world I like... Ina Garten, sweater weather, and... When Muppets present at award shows?
jokes.setup: Liz and Carol bonding over TV
jokes.explanation: Great specific absurd shared interest

jokes.text: And don't you agree that our situation is perfect right now? We have these great visits together, but then we still have our separate lives. We're like Jeffrey and Ina
jokes.punchline: Comparing to the Barefoot Contessa again
jokes.explanation: Continues the Barefoot Contessa metaphor

jokes.text: I'm not like Jeffrey Garten. I'm not as strong as that guy!
jokes.setup: Liz comparing them to Jeffrey and Ina
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of the Barefoot Contessa metaphor

jokes.text: How many women have you been with? I'd rather not say. It's shameful. I mean, I'm a pilot... Six
jokes.setup: Building up to a shocking number
jokes.punchline: The surprisingly low number for a pilot
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection subverting pilot stereotypes

jokes.text: Six.
jokes.punchline: Surprisingly low number for a pilot
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection - expects high number but reveals Carol's actually inexperienced, great economy

jokes.text: Oh, that's not so great
jokes.explanation: Good Liz social awkwardness

jokes.text: I had to spoon him for, like, an hour. And I was the outer spoon
jokes.explanation: Good relationship role reversal humor

jokes.text: her anger eventually transforming into some rather interesting sex
jokes.punchline: The clinical description of anger sex
jokes.explanation: Jack's formal awkward oversharing

jokes.text: It has to be elk tongue!
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the elk tongue paint

jokes.text: And this morning, I made love to my wife. And she was still asleep, so I didn't have to be gentle
jokes.setup: Pete talking about his free time
jokes.explanation: Dark Pete humor that's uncomfortable

jokes.text: Are you sure? Think about it again.
jokes.setup: Lemon says it's the most upsetting thing
jokes.punchline: Pete encourages her to really consider it
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Pete's obliviousness to how disturbing his comment was

jokes.text: That's one of the most upsetting things I have ever imagined. Are you sure? Think about it again
jokes.setup: Liz reacts to Pete's disturbing comment
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of discomfort

jokes.text: it's cheaper for us to just replace anyone who gets murdered
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark corporate logic

jokes.text: His name is James. Not Jim, not Jimmy... Jamessss
jokes.punchline: The emphasized pronunciation implying he's gay
jokes.explanation: Sets up Jack's plan to seduce James

jokes.text: Have you seen my eyes, Lemon? Yep. They're very blue. Like a Mykonos sky
jokes.explanation: Good Jack vanity with specific detail

jokes.text: Mark Foley once called them 'piercing'
jokes.explanation: Dark political reference about disgraced congressman

jokes.text: Do you know what a prize I am in the gay community? There's a term for it. I'm a bear... And I'm a daddy bear
jokes.explanation: Good Jack ego applied to gay culture

jokes.text: Not a GLAAD award, I know that
jokes.setup: Jack talking about what he'll get from flirting
jokes.explanation: Meta joke about gay media representation

jokes.text: Or would you rather teach your cat to dial 911?
jokes.explanation: Great absurd imagery about Liz's loneliness

jokes.text: And second of all, you know that I have Life Alert
jokes.punchline: Liz revealing she has Life Alert
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz pathetic revelation

jokes.text: Would an imaginary me know that you have a mole on your list of pets to get? Or that your favorite color is rainbow?
jokes.setup: Kenneth proving he's real to Tracy
jokes.punchline: The bizarre personal details about Tracy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy absurdity revealed through Kenneth

jokes.text: Of course it would. It would know anything I knew because it sprung from my imagination
jokes.explanation: Surprising Tracy logic about imagination

jokes.text: Why don't you come back home to TGS and pick the peas out of my fried rice? And the rice. I just want carrots
jokes.setup: Tracy trying to lure Kenneth back
jokes.explanation: Classic Tracy weird food demands

jokes.text: But don't you miss rubbing my foot back into the shape of a foot?
jokes.setup: Tracy listing things Kenneth might miss
jokes.explanation: Perfectly disturbing Tracy detail

jokes.text: They gave me a tote bag with The Mentalist on it!
jokes.setup: Kenneth explaining why he's happy at CBS
jokes.explanation: Good Kenneth simple pleasures humor

jokes.text: Obviously, I'm going to need the tote bag
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy entitlement

jokes.text: Do you see something, uh, here that you like?
jokes.setup: Jack trying to seduce the interior designer
jokes.explanation: Jack's uncomfortable seduction attempt

jokes.text: There's one too many producers, Pete. Okay, I get it. But please... I have five kids... That I don't want to be at home with
jokes.punchline: His honest admission about his kids
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pete honesty about family life

jokes.text: the last time I said that, I was in a three-way with two of the Backstreet Boys
jokes.explanation: Classic Jenna inappropriate oversharing

jokes.text: why does Geico have three different spokespeople? They have the caveman, the lizard, and then the stack of money with the eyeballs
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about advertising

jokes.text: And the fake Rod Serling guy
jokes.explanation: Good addition to the Geico observation

jokes.text: I'm on a waiting list to adopt a kid. Touched by a priest... it's fine
jokes.setup: Sharing personal secrets on count of three
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark simultaneous reveal with great timing

jokes.text: Is that what 'ragazzi robusti' means?
jokes.punchline: Liz realizing what the Italian means
jokes.explanation: Good foreign language reveal

jokes.text: Hannibal defeated Fabian with a decoy army. James was a plant. The strie wall finish was a decoy. She Hannibaled my Fabian!
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback and escalation, perfect structure

jokes.text: Like... Whiskey and hunting
jokes.setup: Jack describing how perfect he and Avery are
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack masculine pairing

jokes.text: She's your pube shirt
jokes.explanation: Great callback to would-you-rather game

jokes.text: Good night, bear! Good night moon... Soon Park from accounting
jokes.setup: Kenneth doing Goodnight Moon style sign-off
jokes.explanation: Clever Kenneth wordplay with children's book reference

tmdb_overview: Jack begins to meddle in Liz's new relationship with Carol.
insight: Season premiere reboots with 99 jokes in 24 minutes—peak joke density carries Liz through a reinvention arc.
